title: Keel Lorenz and Third Impact
wikitext:
[]: Keel Lorenz and Third Impact             Quote Post by Ultrahuntr » Mon Oct 20, 2014 12:28 am Alright, so I'm interested in discussing this a little bit more than the wiki cover it. The wiki says "At some point between Second Impact and 2015, most of Keel's body, below the neck, was replaced with cybernetic implants. Although he uses a visor to see, Keel still possesses eyes (as indicated by production drawings). When the organic components of his body became LCL, the remaining mechanical components were left behind, and appear to include most of his spinal column." 1) Where's the source for when this occurred (just curious). I know 15 years is a huge time frame, but I'm wondering if there's more information to be found about this. 2) Are the other members of Seele also part machine? This point has the potential to completely change my view of Seele as a whole. 3)What are your opinions on this?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Oct 20, 2014 12:34 am Exactly when he became a cyborg is entirely speculation, but he notably doesn't have the visor in the photos of him immediately after 2I, so it's feasible that he might be entirely human at that point. We know that he's a cyborg later on because, in EoE, there's all sorts of weird shit left over after his organic parts melt. No information is provided on the other Seele members. There's nothing suggesting it nor denying it. If you want to make them part-machine in a fanfic, go right ahead.

[]: Re: Keel Lorenz and Third Impact             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Oct 20, 2014 12:35 am Ultrahuntr wrote: 1) Where's the source for when this occurred (just curious). I know 15 years is a huge time frame, but I'm wondering if there's more information to be found about this.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Mon Oct 20, 2014 1:35 am I imagine someone found out about the plan for third impact and tried to assassinate him.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Oct 20, 2014 2:42 am Or maybe he was just getting very old and/or got very sick (like cancer) and wouldn't had survived until 3I without cybernetic part replacing his failing organs, and he really wanted to be present for Instrumentality. (especially if their true plan follows the one depicted in the NGE2 videogame, with SEELE sacrificing all of mankind to elevate themselves into eternal beings.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Mon Oct 20, 2014 2:46 am He was missing the lower half of his body!

[]:             Quote Post by Ultrahuntr » Mon Oct 20, 2014 11:29 am It's possible though that in order to make room for the machinery that would keep him alive he had to give up his legs/hips. This makes sense since after he's tanged you hear a motor sputtering. Maybe his life support system was actually quite large.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Oct 20, 2014 1:40 pm Not sure what the problem there was exactly, but I fixed your image.

[]:             Quote Post by Ultrahuntr » Mon Oct 20, 2014 3:00 pm NemZ wrote: Not sure what the problem there was exactly, but I fixed your image.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Oct 20, 2014 3:27 pm Just asked for a new image link. Not sure what was wrong with the first one, but oh well.

[]:             Quote Post by Ultrahuntr » Mon Oct 20, 2014 3:30 pm NemZ wrote: Just asked for a new image link. Not sure what was wrong with the first one, but oh well.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Oct 20, 2014 3:40 pm are you clicking on the 'sharing options' button or just grabbing a link from the address bar? They aren't the same.

[]:             Quote Post by Ultrahuntr » Mon Oct 20, 2014 3:48 pm I was clicking the wrong share link. I know which one I need now. Glad I got that figured out haha. Thanks for pointing that out.

[]:             Quote Post by SolidEye » Tue Oct 21, 2014 11:06 pm Seele always has been ambiguous about their ambitions, origin and especially their Members. Basically it is up to speculation, on exactly how Keel was brought into this condition. It may be the result of something like a contact experiment with the mass production evas, which resulted into the decay of half his body, or the organic parts might be related to the magi system. Also Gendo wears a similar visor in NTE, which might hint that he is in a quite akin situation.

[]:             Quote Post by Ultrahuntr » Thu Oct 23, 2014 4:30 pm Possibly. I just wish there was a little more information on them.

[]:             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Tue Oct 28, 2014 8:59 pm Do we ever even see his full body in the show? I don't remember seeing him anywhere other than at a table with the SEELE monoliths.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Tue Oct 28, 2014 9:24 pm This makes that weird "Ewiger Jude" nonsense the wiki mentions all the stranger, since obviously someone cursed to live to the end of the world would have no purpose or desire for mechanical life extension. Unless Jesus went around zapping people into being cyborgs. Which, given Eva's penchant for odd usages of religious elements, might not seem implausible to someone with the notion that Eva has some truly esoteric meaning or such. But whence came that story anyway? What was the first record of it? I do have the headcanon that Kiel keeps the Dead Sea Scrolls in hollow compartments in his spine, if that's at all of interest to anyone.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Oct 28, 2014 9:30 pm El Squibbonator wrote: Do we ever even see his full body in the show? I don't remember seeing him anywhere other than at a table with the SEELE monoliths.
